nodes.title: Browser
nodes.purpose: The visitor's browser issues GET and POST requests and receives the rendered HTML response. It is the only client; SousMeow ships no separate front-end app.

nodes.title: Front controller
nodes.purpose: Every request that is not a static file enters one PHP entry point, which sets the security headers and Content-Security-Policy, strips the base path, and hands the request to routing.

files.reason: The single front controller every dynamic request passes through before anything else runs.

nodes.title: Auth & CSRF gate
nodes.purpose: Authenticates the session and verifies a CSRF token on every unsafe (state-changing) request. This is the one chokepoint where request security is enforced.

files.reason: The single CSRF gate the front controller verifies on every POST.
Provides requireLogin / requireVerified / requireAdmin used to authorise protected actions.

nodes.title: Controller
nodes.purpose: The matched controller handles the request — coordinating models and services and choosing what to render. The Runner is the busiest example.

files.reason: The core-loop controller; shown here as the representative request handler.
The complete route table mapping method+path to the controller action that handles it.

nodes.title: PDO data path
nodes.purpose: The single database-access path used for every read and write. It targets SQLite in development and MySQL in production behind one interface.

files.reason: The one PDO wrapper; prepared-statement helpers and transactions live here and nowhere else.

nodes.title: Escaped view
nodes.purpose: Renders the HTML response with all dynamic output escaped before it leaves the application, so pasted or stored content can never inject markup.

files.reason: Escapes untrusted content first, then applies a small Markdown allowlist to the escaped text.

nodes.title: Admin (CLI only)
nodes.purpose: Seeding and content management run from the command line, never over HTTP. This path deliberately bypasses the web routes and the CSRF gate.

files.reason: The CLI seeder that applies the schema and upserts the catalog; the only admin write path.

nodes.uncertainty: The seeder is inferred-from-source; its internals were not line-traced.

edges.label: sends request
edges.explanation: The browser sends every dynamic HTTP request to the single front controller.

files.reason: Receives the request and begins security + routing.

edges.label: validates request
edges.explanation: The front controller authenticates the session and validates the CSRF token on every unsafe method before the request may proceed.

files.reason: Invokes the CSRF verification on every POST.
Performs the token check that gates state-changing requests.

edges.label: routes to controller
edges.explanation: Once the gate passes, the router matches the path against the route table and dispatches to the matching controller action.

files.reason: The verified table that determines which controller a path reaches.
Performs the actual match and dispatch — the step that is still inferred.

edges.basis: The route table (app/routes.php) is verified from source, but the Router class matching itself was not line-traced.
edges.uncertainty: Treat routing edge cases (parameter parsing, 404/405 handling) as inferred until the Router is traced.

edges.label: reads & writes via PDO
edges.explanation: The controller performs every database read and write through the single PDO data path, never issuing SQL directly.

files.reason: Reads and writes artifacts and projects during the core loop.
The only persistence path every model is built on.

edges.label: renders escaped HTML
edges.explanation: The controller renders an HTML view with all dynamic output escaped before the response is returned to the browser.

files.reason: Escapes any stored or pasted content the view includes.

edges.label: writes directly (CLI)
edges.explanation: Admin seeding and management run from the CLI and write through the same PDO path directly, bypassing every web route and the CSRF gate.

files.reason: Applies the schema and upserts the catalog straight through the database layer.

edges.basis: scripts/seed.php is inferred-from-source; the CLI write path is declared but its execution was not fully traced.
edges.uncertainty: The exact upsert queries were not line-traced.
